docs.title: equip Plugin Manager Implementation Plan
headings: Task 1: Enums, constants, errors, manifest v3
Marker embedded in every vendored `.claude/**.md`, distinct from
GENERATED_SENTINEL: a vendored file is an upstream copy, not our render.
Capability inference for discovered plugins: free-text tokens (plugin name,
description, keywords, category) -> a canonical Capability. Broader than the
proposal table — discovery WANTS implement/test/review hits because a user
explicitly asked to find tools. First match in order wins per capability.
Marketplace-entry keys that declare a code-running surface, mapped to the
PluginSurface they imply. Inert surfaces (agents/skills/commands) need no
special handling — their absence from this map is the point.
Task 6: Sources I/O (subprocess, behind a Runner seam)
Fall back to a plain newline list parse (tests use this shape).
Task 8: Export public surface + vendoring helper
untrusted + code-running -> must be flagged as needing approval
Task 10: Lifecycle — status/uninstall cover MARKETPLACE + VENDORED, and the VENDOR install path

docs.title: Parallel Council Dispatch Implementation Plan
headings: Task 2: Relocate dev-pick I/O helpers into the domain layer
--- feature/manifest I/O (relocated from cli/dev_pick.py so domains own it) --
dev subagent_type resolved via pick_dev (Senior Developer fallback here)
CRITIQUE handled in Task 5; placeholder keeps this task's tests green.
Task 5: Critique-stage roster expansion + agent-bounded cap
deep critique = 3 agents/feature; cap=4 ⇒ only the first feature fits
Deterministic critic roster by mode: (role, subagent_type) pairs.
light = no critique; standard = one critic (security, the most universal);
deep = all three. Replaces per-feature "relevance" judgment with a fixed,
take_positional=False: this verb has no positional path, so the *value*
of `--mode`/`--cap` must not be mistaken for a scope argument (matches
council.py). Root is passed via `--root DIR`.
`--next` and `--tree-enrich` and `--json` are bare flags; strip them out
before kv parsing so they aren't mistaken for `--key value` pairs.
Task 8: Integration test — drive a multi-feature repo to completion
standard active stages are 1,2,3,4 — each must have appeared
frontier is still SPECIFY, and only `b` is dispatched (a already done)
Task 9: Council skill markdown — retire serial loop, add parallel-dispatch procedure

docs.title: Always-on Drift-Triggered Auto-Council — Implementation Plan
headings: Reference: existing code this plan builds on
Task 0: Empirically verify the Stop `decision: block` contract
add to the import block at the top of reconcile_gate.py:
Task 5: Add the gate command to the Stop hook entry (local scope)
global bodies carry the defer-check guard
Guard prefixed onto every GLOBAL hook command so a repo with its own
(--local) dummyindex hooks — the per-repo override — suppresses the global
ones. defer-check exits 0 (success) when the project has a local install,
so `&& exit 0` short-circuits before the real command runs.
no local install -> exit 1 (do not defer)
Task 8: Integration test — end-to-end gate over a drifted fixture
Substantial session: fake a transcript with enough output tokens.
Task 9: Docs, help text, CHANGELOG, version bump

docs.title: Parallel council dispatch — design
headings: The one real bottleneck: the council per-feature loop
Approach (chosen): CLI-driven stage batches — the council twin of `--next-wave`
dispatch every unit in `batch` IN PARALLEL — one Task call per unit, all in one message:
- subagent_type per unit (from the batch payload)
- persona body inlined (agents/<persona_id>.md), framework slot filled
failure isolation: a failed unit stays at its stage; record it, do NOT stop the loop
at the end: report any features that never reached completion
